When working with the codebase, please refer to the Understand-Anything knowledge graph located at .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json. This graph provides a comprehensive overview of the architecture, API layers, and dependencies within the project. It is essential to internalize this information to effectively navigate the codebase and answer questions accurately. Avoid attempting to index the entire codebase blindly, as the knowledge graph will guide you to the relevant sections and help you understand the relationships between different components.

When generating commit messages, please refer to the commit message guidelines outlined in .github/instructions/copilot-commit.instructions.md. This document provides best practices for writing clear and concise commit messages that effectively communicate the purpose and impact of each change. Adhering to these guidelines will help maintain a clean and organized commit history, making it easier for other developers to understand the evolution of the codebase and collaborate effectively.
